    {"repo": "github.com/prometheus/prometheus", "path": "promql/engine.go", "symbol": "Eval"},
    {"repo": "github.com/prometheus/prometheus", "path": "promql/functions.go", "symbol": "FunctionCall"}
  ],
  "text": "The PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline in prometheus/prometheus consists of several integrated components:\n\n**Parser Interface & Implementation** (promql/parser/parse.go):\n- The `Parser` interface defines the public API for parsing PromQL queries, with methods like `ParseExpr()`, `ParseMetric()`, and `ParseMetricSelector()`\n- `promQLParser` struct implements the Parser interface and holds parser options\n- Internal `parser` struct manages the parsing state, including the Lexer and yacc parser\n- `ParseExpr()` method parses a PromQL expression string into an AST, followed by type checking via `checkAST()`\n- `checkAST()` performs semantic validation and type checking on the AST nodes, validating expression types, function arguments, operator usage, and vector matching constraints\n\n**Lexical Analysis** (promql/parser/lex.go):\n- `Lexer` struct tokenizes the input query string into Items\n- `Item` struct represents lexical tokens with type (ItemType), position, and value\n- `ItemType` enumeration defines all token types: operators (ADD, SUB, MUL, DIV, etc.), keywords (BY, WITHOUT, ON, etc.), aggregators (SUM, AVG, COUNT, etc.), and special tokens\n- Multiple lexing state functions (lexStatements, lexInsideBraces, lexString, etc.) implement a state machine for tokenization\n- Supports histogram literal descriptions, duration expressions, and range vector syntax\n\n**Abstract Syntax Tree Definitions** (promql/parser/ast.go):\n- `Expr` interface represents all expression types with `Type()` method for value type information\n- Expression node types include: `VectorSelector` (metric selection with label matchers), `BinaryExpr` (arithmetic/logical operations), `AggregateExpr` (aggregation operations), `Call` (function calls), `MatrixSelector` (range vectors), `SubqueryExpr` (subqueries), `NumberLiteral`, `StringLiteral`, `ParenExpr`, `UnaryExpr`\n- `Node` interface is the base for all AST nodes, supporting tree traversal via `Walk()`, `Inspect()`, and `ChildrenIter()` functions\n- `VectorMatching` struct describes how vectors are matched in binary operations (one-to-one, many-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many cardinalities)\n- Position tracking via `PositionRange` for error reporting\n\n**Yacc-Generated Parser** (promql/parser/generated_parser.y and generated_parser.y.go):\n- `generated_parser.y` contains the yacc grammar defining PromQL syntax rules\n- `generated_parser.y.go` is the compiled Go parser generated from the yacc grammar\n- Defines grammar rules for expressions, aggregations, binary operations, function calls, metric selectors, and histogram literals\n- Integrates with the Lexer via the yyLexer interface for token consumption\n- Uses semantic actions to build AST nodes during parsing\n\n**Function Registry** (promql/parser/functions.go):\n- `Function` struct defines function metadata: name, argument types, variadic flag, return type, and experimental status\n- `Functions` map provides registry of all supported PromQL functions (e.g., abs, rate, increase, histogram_quantile, label_join, etc.)\n- `getFunction()` helper retrieves function definitions for validation\n- Type information enables the parser to validate function call argument types during `checkAST()`\n\n**Value Type System** (promql/parser/value.go and promql/value.go):\n- `ValueType` defines the four expression result types: instant vector, range vector, scalar, and string\n- Type validation ensures correct usage: vectors for metric operations, scalars for arithmetic, strings for regex operations\n- `DocumentedType()` converts internal types to user-facing terminology\n- Runtime value types (`Vector`, `Matrix`, `Scalar`, `String`) in promql/value.go correspond to evaluation results\n\n**Query Engine Integration** (promql/engine.go):\n- `Engine` executes parsed and validated PromQL expressions\n- Parser output feeds into the Engine's evaluation pipeline\n- Type information from parsed expressions guides evaluation strategy selection\n- Function implementations in promql/functions.go execute using the parsed AST\n\n**Supporting Components**:\n- `promql/parser/posrange/posrange.go`: Position tracking for error reporting with line:column format\n- `promql/parser/printer.go`: AST to string conversion for debugging\n- `promql/parser/prettier.go`: Formatted query output\n- `promql/parser/features.go`: Feature flag registration for experimental syntax\n\n**Validation Pipeline Flow**:\n1. Input query string → Lexer tokenization\n2. Token stream → Yacc parser builds AST\n3. AST → Type checking and semantic validation via checkAST()\n4. Validated AST → Engine evaluation\n\nThis architecture ensures PromQL queries are syntactically correct, semantically valid, and type-safe before execution."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/prometheus/prometheus` — use `repo:^github.com/prometheus/prometheus$` filter
- `github.com/sourcegraph-testing/prometheus-common` — use `repo:^github.com/sourcegraph-testing/prometheus-common$` filter
- `github.com/prometheus/alertmanager` — use `repo:^github.com/prometheus/alertmanager$` filter
- `github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

**Sourcegraph Repositories:** `github.com/prometheus/prometheus`, `github.com/sourcegraph-testing/prometheus-common`, `github.com/prometheus/alertmanager`, `github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec`

# Prometheus PromQL Query Parsing and Validation Pipeline

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in prometheus/prometheus that implement the PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline. Identify: the Parser interface and parser struct with ParseExpr and checkAST (in promql/parser/), the Lexer tokenizer, the AST node type definitions (Expr, VectorSelector, BinaryExpr, etc.), the generated yacc parser, the Function registry defining valid PromQL functions, the value type system used for expression type validation, and the Engine that integrates the parser.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: prometheus/prometheus.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `prometheus/prometheus` (prometheus/prometheus)
- `sourcegraph-testing/prometheus-common` (prometheus/client_golang)
- `prometheus/alertmanager` (prometheus/alertmanager)
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all Go source files in the prometheus/prometheus repository that implement the PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and identifying the key components.
Now let me read the functions registry and value type system:
Let me read the engine and check the generated parser files:
Let me search for more key files in the parser package:
Let me check the posrange package and look for other related files:
Now let me search for any other key components I might have missed:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the PromQL parsing and validation pipeline. Let me create the answer file:

Perfect! I've successfully identified and documented all the key Go source files and components that implement the PromQL query parsing and validation pipeline in prometheus/prometheus. 

## Summary

I found **14 essential Go source files** across the promql package that implement the complete parsing and validation pipeline:

**Core Parser Components:**
- `promql/parser/parse.go` - Parser interface and implementation with ParseExpr() and checkAST() methods
- `promql/parser/ast.go` - AST node definitions (Expr, VectorSelector, BinaryExpr, AggregateExpr, Call, etc.)
- `promql/parser/lex.go` - Lexer tokenizer with Item and ItemType definitions
- `promql/parser/generated_parser.y[.go]` - Yacc grammar and compiled parser

**Function & Type System:**
- `promql/parser/functions.go` - Function registry with 80+ PromQL functions
- `promql/parser/value.go` - Value type system (vector, matrix, scalar, string)

**Engine Integration:**
- `promql/engine.go` - Query execution engine
- `promql/functions.go` & `promql/value.go` - Runtime value types and function implementations

**Supporting Components:**
- `promql/parser/posrange/posrange.go` - Error position tracking
- `promql/parser/printer.go`, `prettier.go`, `features.go` - AST output formatting and feature registration

The complete analysis has been written to `/workspace/answer.json` with file references, key symbols, execution chain, and a detailed narrative explaining the parsing pipeline architecture.

map provides registry of all supported PromQL functions (e.g., abs, rate, increase, histogram_quantile, label_join, etc.)\n- `getFunction()` helper retrieves function definitions for validation\n- Type information enables the parser to validate function call argument types during `checkAST()`\n\n**Value Type System** (promql/parser/value.go and promql/value.go):\n- `ValueType` defines the four expression result types: instant vector, range vector, scalar, and string\n- Type validation ensures correct usage: vectors for metric operations, scalars for arithmetic, strings for regex operations\n- `DocumentedType()` converts internal types to user-facing terminology\n- Runtime value types (`Vector`, `Matrix`, `Scalar`, `String`) in promql/value.go correspond to evaluation results\n\n**Query Engine Integration** (promql/engine.go):\n- `Engine` executes parsed and validated PromQL expressions\n- Parser output feeds into the Engine's evaluation pipeline\n- Type information from parsed expressions guides evaluation strategy selection\n- Function implementations in promql/functions.go execute using the parsed AST\n\n**Supporting Components**:\n- `promql/parser/posrange/posrange.go`: Position tracking for error reporting with line:column format\n- `promql/parser/printer.go`: AST to string conversion for debugging\n- `promql/parser/prettier.go`: Formatted query output\n- `promql/parser/features.go`: Feature flag registration for experimental syntax\n\n**Validation Pipeline Flow**:\n1. Input query string → Lexer tokenization\n2. Token stream → Yacc parser builds AST\n3. AST → Type checking and semantic validation via checkAST()\n4. Validated AST → Engine evaluation\n\nThis architecture ensures PromQL queries are syntactically correct, semantically valid, and type-safe before execution."
}
